name: Low Carb Diet
tagline: Trim excess fat while making slow lean gains
ideal: Anyone new to structured dieting. Insulin-resistant individuals.
description: This diet keeps carbs low and fats moderate-to-high. Fats help with satiety, keeping you full even in a deficit. It takes time to show results but is very sustainable.
tips: Start with a 200-300 calorie deficit for the first 2 weeks
Never cut more than 1,000 calories from TDEE
Fats in this diet help you stay full — don't fear them
Good for insulin-resistant individuals and beginners

meals.title: Breakfast (~350 kcal)
items: 1 cup spinach sautéed in ½ tbsp olive oil
½ cup oats OR skip for stricter low-carb

meals.title: Lunch (~480 kcal)
items: 1 Roti (reduce if not hitting fat loss goals)

meals.title: Pre/Post Workout (~250 kcal)
items: Handful of almonds or walnuts (good fats + satiety)

meals.title: Dinner (~420 kcal)
items: Small portion of dal for protein + fibre

name: Zone Diet
tagline: Higher carbs — ideal for those with higher muscle mass
ideal: People who love carbs and have high carb tolerance. Those with more muscle mass.
description: The Zone Diet has a higher carbohydrate ratio. It is ideal for people with a high tolerance to carbs — typically those with more muscle mass. Keep carb sources clean and time them around your workout.
tips: Consume carbs 1-2 hours before your workout for best performance
Use oats, brown rice, and sweet potato as your carb sources
Stay in a small calorie surplus for lean muscle gains
In a calorie deficit, this diet also works for fat loss

meals.title: Breakfast (~450 kcal)
items: 60g oats cooked with water or skim milk
1 scoop whey protein mixed in OR 3 whole eggs on side

meals.title: Lunch (~550 kcal)
items: 150g Paneer or Soya Chunks in sabzi

meals.title: Pre-Workout (~300 kcal)
items: 1 medium banana or 1 sweet potato (quick carbs for energy)

meals.title: Dinner (~500 kcal)
items: 1 small bowl of rice (post-workout carb replenishment)

name: Ketogenic Diet
tagline: Force your body to burn fat as its primary fuel
ideal: Those who struggle with adherence on carb-based diets. Strict fat-loss goal.
description: A ketogenic diet forces the body into ketosis — a state where it burns fat and ketones instead of glucose. Keep carbs below 20-30g per day. It takes 2-3 days to enter ketosis. Do NOT use a refeed until after at least 4 continuous weeks.
tips: Keep carbs below 20-30g per day — even vegetables count
Ketosis kicks in after 2-3 days — push through the first week
If you lose 1-2kg in 2-3 days, it's water weight from glycogen depletion
Do NOT have a refeed for at least 4 continuous weeks
Higher protein early in adaptation; reduce to 15-20% once in ketosis
A cheat meal can completely reverse ketosis — stay strict

meals.title: Breakfast (~400 kcal)
items: 4 egg whites OR 2 whole eggs scrambled in coconut oil

meals.title: Lunch (~500 kcal)
items: 1 bowl spinach or dark green vegetables
NO rice, NO roti, NO fruits

meals.title: Snack (~250 kcal)
items: Paneer cubes sautéed in extra virgin coconut oil

meals.title: Dinner (~450 kcal)
items: 1 tbsp flaxseed or handful of nuts
